column3: Intestinal Issues
column4: I am a young man age 19. Before this illness I was 197 lb at 6'0 15% bodyfat. I was very much into lifting and I loved it. 10 months ago I started to get nauseated whenever I ate, and it progressively got worse. Soon I was down to eating 1 meal everyday since it was all I could stomach without throwing up. Needless to say I began to lose weight rapidly; this sickness came with a whole host of symptoms, constipation, abdominal pain, fatigue, weight loss, brain fog, gas, bloating, dysbiosis, and worst of all, the discomfort. I do not believe the feeling of discomfort is caused by the constipation or intestinal bloating, and I am not sure what causes it. However it is an indescribably bad feeling, worse than any pain I have felt or will feel for the rest of my life. The fatigue was to the point where when I stood up my whole body would tingle and my vision would go dark, I almost collapsed every time I would stand and had to brace myself or I would fall. I have defecated blood once, probably caused by severe constipation, and never again. I have had an ultrasound, a CT scan, multiple blood tests, an upper endoscopy with a stomach biopsy and numerous other small tests. No doctor has been able to conclusively diagnose me, and no medication has worked for me despite seeing many doctors and trying many prescriptions.   Before any of this, I was the pinnacle of health among my friends, family and community. Last month I weighed in at 142 lb. Many doctors have misdiagnosed me, and here are some examples. I do not have Crohns, UC, Autoimmune Gastrointestinal Dysmotility, Stomach Ulcers, Diverticulitis, Gastroenteritis. I have given up going to doctors and I am currently seeing a holistic practitioner who is responsible for my current diet and well being. He instructed me to go on a meat and broth diet, eating nothing but meat and organ tissue and drinking bone broth. Being a nutrition and dietetics student, I questioned his judgement, as such diets can cause constipation on top of my current constipation. I followed his instructions and I feel more normal now than I have in the past 10 months and have gained weight to 153 lb; to give a number I feel 70% better. I am going to see him within the next few days and hopefully he will add carbs of vegetables to this expensive diet. The current most possible diagnosis is SIBO but if anyone reading this thinks otherwise please share your belief. Any words of advice or similar illnesses are greatly appreciated. Thank you very much for taking the time to read my post. Stay healthy

column3: Neck muscle flexing, possibly Omohyoid, anytime I move my head
column4: 29 years old, male, 130 pounds,   I have a doctors appointment on Friday, but within the last two days I\'92ve notice this small thin muscle, running seemingly under my collar bone diagonally under my left Sternomastoid (sorry for spelling) and up my neck into my mandible jaw area,  Flexing and painfully protruding with pretty much any movement of my head, or swallowing, or making faces/brushing my teeth.  Looking to the sides, anything. I\'92ve tried everything, standing, sitting, laying down, shoulders up, shoulder blades back and down, anything, but it\'92s still protruding with pretty much any movement.  When I talk it spasms wildly, and I can sort of see the other end (under my jaw) twitching as well.  Previously I\'92d noticed this sometimes when lifting weights, but I stopped doing that over a year and a half ago. I feel like I might have a problem with my left shoulder/clavicle, but I\'92m not sure what and have been trying to exercise it lightly.   Recently I got a standing desk, to try and fix my posture, but as soon as I did I notice this happening.  It\'92s starting to really effect my life, in a way that honestly has been exhausting.   Not being able to move my face/shoulder/neck without fearing im gonna tear this muscle has me staying in bed, on my back, for most of the day.  It\'92s VERY thin, maybe the width of a normal vein in the arm.   I\'92m just sort of out of ideas for what to do, and really starting to lose it a bit  EDIT: I\'92m noticing now it\'92s happening fairly severely when I move my mouth- smile, make an \'93Ooooh\'94 shape. Particularly if I open my jaw and make sort of an \'93Urrrr\'94 shape, the entire muscle twitches severely from my collarbone, all the way up under my Sternomastoid and into my under-jaw.

column3: 29/F Brown urine & flu-like symptoms
column4: About 5 or 6 months ago I became sick with what seemed like a bad stomach flu or bug. I couldn't eat anything and when I did, it immediately gave me diarrhea. My urine was brown (like Coca-Cola) and it lasted for about 4 days.  It seems like I may be getting the same virus/bug again. I had chills and an achy body yesterday so I went to bed around 6pm. Fast-forward to this morning (about 13 hours of sleep later) and I have dark brown urine again. My stomach felt sensitive this morning but I was able to eat oatmeal without any issues. I have swollen lymph nodes and a wicked sore throat but that just feels like a head cold. I'm more worried about the urine. I don't feel thirsty or dehydrated but I am having a hard time drinking since my throat is so sore.   I took probiotics the last time I had this bug and they seemed to help. Should I be seeing a doctor about the urine color? Or is it likely that I am just dehydrated? I am not currently taking any medications or eating any new foods (that I know of) that would make my urine change colors. It is recurring only with the flu-like symptoms.  I am 5'5", 120lbs, Caucasian, symptoms beginning with sore throat on Friday night and flu-like symptoms beginning 24 hours ago.

column3: Question about anti-fungal creams or lotions
column4: Age:31. Male. 6'0", 205#, White, PmHX: HTN (treated with Ace inhibitor), Celexa for GAD/Depression  I have what I think is a fungal infection in my inguinal fold, and I suppose it's from the moisture in the area.  Looking up general tips for treatment, I am sure to change all of my clothes frequently, clean my sheets after starting an antifungal cream/powder, etc.    However, since so much of the advice given is to "keep the area dry," I wonder if applying an anti-fungal cream (and some hydrocortisone cream for symptom control) is counter-productive in some ways.  It stays pretty moist all day long, partly as a result of the creams.  If I were to wash it and dry it every few hours, I'd be removing the medicine, right?  Any advice?

column3: Elevated Lipase and Amylase
column4: Hello,  I've asked this question on here a little while back, but the issue continues so I am going to ask again. I am a 35 year old white male, 6' 3" tall 210 lbs. I found out that my levels were elevated a few months ago. The current medications that I take are .25 mg Xanax as needed, 75 mg Effexor once a day, and omneprazole once a day.  When I got my first blood test they found that they were slightly elevated. I got the blood test because I was having back and side rib pains, which I now believe to be completely unrelated. I ended up getting and abdominal ultrasound as well as X-rays. Both came back fine and the pancreas and all organs looked good. The X-rays did show some bone spurs on my spine as well as arthritis of the spine, so I think that is the cause of the pain. I did another blood test two weeks ago, my Amylase was 247 and Lipase 295. The GI that I say ordered an MRI with and without contrast. I just got the results back and the pancreas and all organs look good. They only thing he noticed is that I have a cyst on one of my kidneys. I have an appointment with him to discuss in two weeks, the results were read to me over the phone by his assistant so she couldn't answer any questions. Does anyone have any ideas of what could be causing elevated levels like that if everything appears ok?

column3: MRI results. Ortho out of town & wont hear back for 2 weeks
column4: I was hoping someone can help with the results from the MRI and if I will possibly be looking at surgery or just a long recovery in the knee brace. This MRI is 3 weeks post dislocation ordered because I cannot lift my leg straight up at all or without the support of a towel and having trouble contracting the muscles.  Thank you in advance.   Age: 31  Sex: F  Height: 5\'920\'94   Weight: 160lbs  Race: Caucasian  Duration of complaint: dislocated on 5/11  Location: Right patella   Any existing relevant medical issues: 2nd dislocation on same knee, also dislocated left patella 2x\'92s and had lateral release done and bone spurs removed   Current medications if any: Advil, Ibuprofen   MRI KNEE RIGHT WO CONTRAST 5/30/2019  10:49AM CDT  COMPARISON :X-RAY OF THE RIGHT KNEE 5/11/30  History: S83.004D: Unspecified dislocation of right patella, subsequent encounter. History of patellar dislocation.  Technique: MRI KNEE RIGHT WO CONTRAST.  Findings:   .The medial & lateral meniscus is intact. The anterior & posterior cruciate ligament is unremarkable. Medial collateral & lateral collateral ligament & extensor mechanisms is normal.   Partial tearing of the medial patellar rectinaculum with bone marrow contusion within the medial portion of the patella with adjacent marrow edema in the lateral femoral condyle. This is characteristics of transient patellar dislocation.   Hypoplastic femoral trochlea with enlargement of the lateral patellar facet.  Lateral patellar tilt. 1 cm of lateral patellar subluxation.   Osteochnodral fracture of the medial patellar facet & median ridge of the patella with loose body in the lateral gutter measuring 1.5 X .04 cm (axial image 12, series 5 & coronal image 14, series 9). Possible loose body within the medial gutter measuring 1.4 x 0.3 cm (axial image 16, series 5). Grade 2 strain of the distal portion of the vastus medialis muscle.   The neurovascular bundle is normal. The popliteus tendon & pes anserine tendons are normal.   Impression:  1. Transient patellar dislocation of right knee.  2. Osteochondral fracture of the medial patellar facet with osteochondral loose body in the lateral gutter measuring 1.5 x 0.4 cm. 3. Second possible intra-articular loose body within the medial gutter measuring 1.4 x 0.3 cm. 4. Femoral trochlear dysplasia of the right knee. This is usually a bilateral process.  5. Partial tearing of the medial patellar retinaculum.

column3: [Photo included] Should I worry about a pinkish bump that grew overnight on my face just below my right eye and hasn\'92t gone away for two weeks?
column4: About me: 21F, Asian ethnicity, 5\'921, 60kg, from SoCal, no past serious diseases, non smoker, non drinker, family history - just diabetes and hypertension  Well, I\'92m already worrying about it anyway.   I initially thought it was a pimple but I see no heads. It\'92s quite firm and was a bit painful to touch but over the days the pain lessened and disappeared. It shrunk a bit in size and grew a bit tender so I tried to pick on it to let the pus out but no pus came out. The next day, it became firm again so I didn\'92t touch it for the next few days.   I got frustrated over it not leaving my face despite shrinking a bit in size (only a biiiit though, I can still feel a firmness to it when I touch it) so I picked on it again and just like before, it became firm again. There was still no pus or head that came out and I only scraped off the top layer of the skin so there\'92s some redness in the middle now.  It\'92s not itchy. There was never a growth or a mole or a nevus or whatever in that spot before\'97 it was just skin. I don\'92t really go out in the sun, I just stay pretty much indoors most of my life (since middle school up to now). Also, barely did I go out the past year considering COVID was looming around. I don\'92t think I\'92m exposed to any harmful chemicals in the past years up til now. I see no veins growing nor is it shiny or clear looking, it may look like it\'92s shiny though because of the flash from my phone camera.  It\'92s just a bit concerning why it isn\'92t going away as quickly as my prior pimples. Could it be just a nasty deep pimple? My anxiety just gets the best of me sometimes and I think of the worst.  Picture, a few moments after I tried to squeeze out the head of my \'93pimple\'94 so the area around it is quite red also: https://scontent.fmnl4-2.fna.fbcdn.net/v/t1.0-9/fr/cp0/e15/q65/156002568_4486997864649729_3258987518579098507_o.jpg?_nc_cat=101&ccb=3&_nc_sid=110474&efg=eyJpIjoidCJ9&_nc_ohc=LnGkqmEaoYAAX9BvHWV&_nc_ht=scontent.fmnl4-2.fna&tp=14&oh=64785c81d7207e9eb9d08d08d0e3d4e4&oe=60640694  EDIT, here\'92s what it looks like two hours later: https://scontent.fmnl4-4.fna.fbcdn.net/v/t1.0-9/fr/cp0/e15/q65/156432474_4487178544631661_4995849651317513699_o.jpg?_nc_cat=102&ccb=3&_nc_sid=110474&efg=eyJpIjoidCJ9&_nc_ohc=O85AAORVCbIAX8J4vvf&_nc_ht=scontent.fmnl4-4.fna&tp=14&oh=9d44619cb5d418a36f397589c286b88e&oe=6064FDEA

column3: Cut flap of skin, remove or let heal?
column4: On Tuesday of last week I sliced my right pointer finger by jumping to grab something from inside a gutter. It cut across my skin and left a flap of skin hanging. My mom said to leave it and it will heal. Well it technically has healed, its mostly reattached, its comepletely numb. I have absolutely no feeling in that part of skin, the rest of my finger is fine, although that area may be swollen. column3: Possibly myopathy, but can't get a diagnosis
column4: 28M | 195 lbs | 5'6" |Chinese-American | Primary complaint: muscle weakness and tiredness | Duration: off and on since October 2018 | No existing medical issues, medications, smoking, drinking, or drugs | No *known* family medical history of muscle issues  &#x200B;  **TL;DR** \\- I experienced extremely painful muscle seizing in 2018 that treated with high doses of steroids. The episodes returned in 2020 and were treated with steroids. I have noticed muscle weakness and tiredness since the 2018 incident. All tests have returned normal, and no one can seem to diagnose my problem.  &#x200B;  Fall 2018:  * Weighed around 180 lbs. | Walked a couple miles each day during my lunch break | Felt generally healthy * In October, I started experiencing muscle cramps in my quads. I self-treated with lots of water, bananas, and foam rolling. The cramps continued for about a week, maybe two. At some point I also experienced burnt orange colored pee that went away within a day. After the two weeks, the cramping started to grow in frequency, duration, and pain - still isolated to my quads. The cramps were triggered sporadically and would occur when sleeping, walking, sitting, etc. * At this point, I went to the doctor. My general physician ran a blood test and found high bilirubin, and very high creatine kinase levels - upper 6000's. She called me back in the next day to run the numbers again, found they had jumped again, and sent me to the hospital. * In the hospital, they asked me constantly if I was doing drugs - I've never done them in my life. They suspected my kidneys and liver - I believe they ran an ultrasound on my kidneys. All tests were normal. After about a week of thinking that it was drugs and I was lying, they finally gave me an MRI on my quads, and ultimately a muscle biopsy (taken from my left quad). The MRI *and* biopsy were normal! While all of these tests continued coming back normal, my CK levels continued to rise to the 30,000s. * The cramping was still sporadic and still isolated to my quads. Episodes would last a couple hours at times. Both legs were affected equally. The episodes would occur around my quads, and I basically had a Tic-Tac-Toe grid for each thigh where I would document the episodes location. It's easily the worst pain I've had in my life. However, when the episodes were not occurring, I felt 100% fine. * Treatment consisted of pain suppressants and an IV with water until the biopsy came back normal and my CK levels reached the 30,000s. Then they blasted me with 80mg of steroids, and all of a sudden, all my symptoms went away completely. They discharged me without a diagnosis and put me on 60mg prednisone, cutting the dose in half every 5 days.  2019  * Throughout 2019, I weighed over 190 lbs. I used to be able to lose weight very easily, especially with a focus on diet and exercise, but was completely unable to lose any of the weight gained immediately after the hospitalization and steroids. My lifestyle had completely returned to normal. I would occasionally feel cramping in my quads, but never a full episode like before. * My muscles weren't really feel weaker, but had noticeably less stamina, especially in my arms. Tasks like moving furniture were not difficult for me, but wore me out. I had attributed this to my weight gain and just growing older. My arms also fall asleep very quickly when above my heart. * I normally donate blood every 8 weeks or so, but in 2019 I was rejected and asked not to give anymore because I had two false-positive tests in a row for Hep-C. * Once in this year, I experienced the burnt orange pee, but it quickly went away.  2020  * I followed up with my general physician, who ran my blood tests again. My CK levels were once again high, and she sent me to a neurologist. * In February, the neurologist ran a lot of blood tests to look for signs of dermatomyositis and other auto-immune myopathies, but all returned normal. * In March, the episodes suddenly reappeared. Same locations, same symptoms. I never got a blood test during this time because of the COVID lockdowns, but my general physician prescribed 20mg prednisone since that's what worked last time. The episodes continued to grow, and she upped the dosage to 60mg. The episodes stopped, and I haven't had one since. * However, since this most recent occurrence, I have been very noticeably weak and tired. For instance, changing a light fixture for a friend made me want to take a nap, and that's not a difficult task. * The general physician has since run more blood work. My CK levels are normal (the steroid likely saw to that), but my bilirubin is still above the normal window - just like it was in every other tests. She ordered a liver ultrasound, which came back... normal!  And that brings us to now. The doctors don't know what else to do. I've been researching myopathies on my own, and it looks like any specialties in the field would require me to travel to a place like Johns Hopkins. But before I make any more decisions, I thought I'd see what Reddit can offer for free. I've already spent over $10,000 to have learned nothing except that steroids treat the symptoms.  THANK YOU to anyone who has actually read my entire case. I'm sorry it's so long, but I am hoping there's some sort of answer in the details.  Edit: Added the TL;DR

column3: Advice on whether or not to get a cane...?
column4: INFO: I'm a 22y/o guy who is admittedly fairly out of shape. I'm not morbidly obese, but I'm definitely overweight. I've known this was an issue for a while, yet due to the stress of college never committed to getting healthier beyond trying to eat as well as I could afford to. I've seen doctors, I don't have arthritis or anything. Most I usually get it a shrug and "just lose some weight".   I've had issues with my knees for a while now. They crack, they're frequently sore, and I tend to stand with them "locked" instead of apparently how you are supposed to stand? But the last year has been the worst. I gained a lot of weight pretty fast during COVID, gaining nearly 50lbs in a couple years. I want to walk, amongst other things, to lose some weight, but my knees have been giving me problems.  Today I probably overdid it, and one of my knees cracked and nearly gave out. It hurts a bit now, but it's okay. Most of my work out plans don't involve a lot of running on treadmills, mostly either walking, biking, push-ups, sit-ups, etc. I want to walk though. Like I really want to walk. Without worrying.  THE ACTUAL QUESTION:  Would it help me or not to get a cane?   I guess in a weird way I'm worried that somehow it won't help me strengthen my knees and will somehow make me reliant on it, kinda like glasses.   I'm also embarrassed that I'm a relatively healthy 22 y/o and I'm considering this but like... I need to get over that.

column3: Itchy, weeping rash on hands
column4: https://imgur.com/a/2UZCeEz  23, caucasian female, 250lbs, 5\'927\'94, occasional drinker, daily smoker. No known allergies, no regular medications.  I\'92ve had occasional flares of itchy patches on my hands since June last year. Very few and far between, it\'92s only appeared 4-5 times, but this time is the worst.   They start with itchy red patches. I do my best not to scratch but have very low impulse control most of the time. All it takes is a second of contact with anything and the skin atop these patches sloughs off, leaving a raw, itchy, irritated area that weeps a clear/yellow fluid. They then crust over with an almost bubbled appearance.   If I don\'92t scratch the areas they eventually swell, blister and weep on their own, also resulting in the same crusted over bubbled appearance.   These spots only ever appear on my hands - predominately on my fingers, sometimes on the tops of my hands, never on the palms or wrists.   At the present moment I have 4 small spots on my left hand and 4 on my right hand which are much larger.   The only thing I can think of that has occurred at the same time as all these flares has been stress, but unsure if that can really cause something like this. I have seen a doctor about this in the past who said it was more than likely an allergic reaction, however I have no other symptoms of a reaction.

column3: Unusual weight gain over past year
column4: Hello,  * 21 years old * Male * 5'8" * 195lbs * White * USA * No medications  In December 2014 I weighed 155lbs. I had been around 150lbs for the 3 years previous, and havent had any weight issues in my life.  In January 2015 I came down with Chicken Pox. Go figure right? Yes, I was vaccinated when I was a child, but still managed to catch it, although not so severe. Here we are, a year later and I've manage to put on 40lbs.   It's been a gradual, consistent gain. I didnt even notice it right away, until I had trouble fitting it some of my clothes. Once I hit 190 it really started to get me thinking. I haven't changed anything in my lifestyle over the past year to merit this time of extreme change. I started watching my calories and I'm moderately active. I'm the last person to blame my weight on a 'health issue' but now Im almost 200lbs and I'm genuinely concerned.  I dont know if it is a coincidence but it seemed to start when I got Chicken Pox last year. That's the only thing I can think of. I know this isnt much info to go off of.   I scheduled a doc appointment next week, but wanted to see if anyone might have any thoughts or suggestions, or maybe something I should ask my doctor about? Thanks.

column3: 40F, over past few weeks have developed near excruciating groin / hip pain after long walks (leisurely walking for 1-2 hours). GP appointment in 2 days to discuss.
column4: -40/F/5'5"/115 lbs/white -Very little drinking, no smoking -ADHD/Depression (treated with SSRI, Stim, rTMS) -Family history of arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis  This started about 4-5 weeks ago. At first I thought it was due simply to more activity after being sedentary, but I am beginning to question this, particularly after yesterday when the pain became so extreme (and lingers until today).  After about an hour of leisurely walking, I start to feel some tension along the groin area - sort of in the middle. As it would have it, yesterday my phone died and I couldn't call a cab to go home, so I had to take public transit, which involved some more walking, causing tension to turn to pain.  After I rested at home, the pain felt very deep in my groin, and began to throb. Getting up to walk to the bathroom to get an Advil, the pain increased enormously... particularly in my left groin / hip. It was too painful to maintain regular range of motion, and I had to sort of hobble to the medicine cabinet and back to the couch. The Advil helped, but the deep ache lasted for quite a while, and there is some remaining this afternoon.  This is the fourth time I am experiencing this after long-ish walks, but this was the worst. I've had good walking shoes on each time, so feel like that shouldn't be an issue.  Is there any sort of test I should expect / ask my GP for? Or, is there any other information that might be valuable to have handy?   Many thanks.

column3: Is there something wrong with my immune system?
column4: (21F, 5\'926, 170lbs, white, asthma, anemia, I take Wellbutrin and Cymbalta for OCD, I don\'92t drink or smoke at all because of a family history of addiction)   Hello, Redditors. I am here because I think there may be something wrong with my immune system, but I also don\'92t want to bring it up to my doctor unless it\'92s a legitimate issue. Each year, around Spring, I get sick with something that closely resembles mono. Fever (101-103F), chills, aches, no appetite, severe exhaustion, etc., that last for about a month. When I was 8, I did actually have mono, so my doctor always says it\'92s just a virus that\'92s going around because I can\'92t get mono again. The big red flag happened last year when I got sick, and I tested positive for mono. My doctor said I had zero antibodies against the disease and I must have had something else when I was 8, despite the fact that my medical records clearly show I had mono before. I was also sick constantly as a child. I got the flu every year, including h1n1. I would get strep throat sometimes up to 9x in a year until I got my tonsils and adenoids out. A smaller red flag is that whenever I get a blood tests, what ever physician that reads the test tells me that my white blood cell count is high and they ask if I\'92m sick or if I have an infection. When I haven\'92t and I tell them no, they say I must have been sick but not realized it. Is this something to be concerned about? Should I be more adamant about my doctor looking into my immune system? I don\'92t know if I\'92m just being a worry worm or if something is up.  Edit: also worth mentioning is that when I get sick, it takes me forever to get better. For example, I got bronchitis in September, and it didn\'92t start to get better until the end of December.

column3: Donated plasma. String of clotted blood came out if my arm. Questions enclosed.
column4: Male, 163 lbs. 28 yrs. 5'10"  Ok so yesterday I donated plasma for the first time in years. All my life my veins have been tricky for medical professionals so I go into things like this with much patience. First one of the staff tried on the center vein in my left arm. Blood was flowing slowly, varrying between orange and red on their machine (slow and really slow). Guy comes back to fix it, works needle around, no luck. Senior staff member comes over to fix it, very gently drives needle in much deeper. No pain no swelling. Can't remember the term but needle hasn't gone through the vein yet. Helps for a bit but bloodflow slows again and starts clotting in hose.  They switch to right arm. Works fairly well for the rest of donation, but still slow boodflow at times. Donation took over 1 1/2 hrs. Keep in mind I have a bandage on my left arm for this whole time. Now for the strange part...  When I'm done I take the bandage off my left arm. When I do...stuck to the bandage is a clotted string of blood leading into my arm. As I pull the bandage off I pull about two and a half inches of blood string out of my arm along with it. And immediately the area swells up to a marble and starts gushing blood. Swelling went away quickly. About ten minutes, just exploded then disappeared. All that night the fingers in my left hand would tingle then stop, tingling then stop, repeat, etc.  So here are my questions. I'm more curious than concerned everything is fine now. But what the hell happened in my arm? How did blood clot inside of my arm? Why the immediate swelling, then disappeared just as quickly. What caused the tingling? How bad is it if clotted blood gets into the bloodstream?    TL;DR String of clotted blood came out my arm and gushing blood followed. How did this happen? How bad is this, or could it have been? What happened in my arm?  Edit: I should add I was dehydrated. I guess I was close to the limit of being too dehydrated to donate. Which is strange since I've been drinking plenty of water, and haven't had coffee or alcohol for over a week. I'm sure that led to my blood being quick to clot, but HOW did it clot in my vein?

column3: How do I get doctors to treat me seriously? (Stomach issues)
column4: Age: 24 Sex: Female Height: 5'7" Weight: 220 Race: White Duration of complaint: About 4 years now Location: GI tract Existing Medical Issues: Depression, Anxiety, Vitamin D deficient Current medication: None  So for the past 4 years I've been having stomach issues. I've been pooping/vomiting blood, have had intense pain, felt nauseous/pain after eating, and my poop is just always softer than what it used to be (feels like I spend like 5 minutes wiping most of the time). Now all of these symptoms are not always constant besides the last one.  The only medical professional who took me seriously is a doctor who I had to stop seeing because the medical bills from an endoscopy and colonoscopy where p.expensive. He did find out that I don't have any food allergies (not dairy or gluten), my stomach is very prone to ulcers but I don't have acid reflux.   I've since moved and now live an 11 hour drive away from the state I used to see that doctor in, so he definitely isn't an option anymore. I've been to hospitals around here when it gets really bad, and they don't seem to take me seriously. They just shove me out asap under the principle that they can't do anything for me. I find that to be kinda bullshit as I'm sure they have the same equipment as that doctor I used to see and then some. I'm not looking for pain meds, but for an answer.  It's been pondered if I have an IBD or immune issue. My eyes tend to be very sensitive to light (I do wear glasses, my eye doctor never saw any sign disease in them), and I have issues with my joints such as being able to easily dislocate my fingers and kinda toes and have issues with pain with them as well.  But for real, how do I get doctors to believe me without flat out saying I am sick with something I might actually not be sick with? I'm afraid of trying to find a normal doctor in this new state due to them not believing me so far.

I thought it might be strep throat, so I went to the doctor yesterday, and the test came back negative.  So far, I\'92m convinced it\'92s just a bad cold, but this morning I woke up with hot red splotchy rashes on my wrists, feet, knees and back of my hands. They are not itchy, just raised and hot. I took an oatmeal bath, and they are still persisting.  See [here.](https://imgur.com/a/tEkLx8V)  I also have a few little red raised bumps that are itchy, and seem like insect bites. (Distinctly different from the raised rash.)   At this point, I\'92m more concerned about the rashes than the illness symptoms, as I don\'92t have any known allergies. Could they be related to the fever and other symptoms I\'92ve been having? Is it worth it to do in to the doctor again, or just take some Benadryl and hope they go away?  Edit: the rash extends up into the palms of my hands, in case the photos do not show it properly.

column3: Could This All Be From A Hiatal Hernia?
column4: Im 31, 6"2 220lbs, white, male, no drinking or smoking or drugs, diagnosed conditions are a hiatal hernia, Barrett's Esophagus, previous high cholesterol.  This has gone on for 4 years really, but has been getting worse, I was diagnosed with a "medium" sized hiatal hernia 3 years ago.  I basically feel sick, weak and/or tired all the time, one of my primary complaints is there is a frequent sick feeling in my lower chest and upper stomach, when this really acts I feel weak, sick, I feel my heart beating, its hard to tell what even sets it off. This was happening some 3 years ago but I feel its worse now, back then I had an upper endoscopy, abdominal ultrasound, abdominal ct scan, which only really showed the hiatal hernia and Barrett's, and I had a non continous 30 day heart monitor and an echocardiogram which showed nothing wrong. I have had a blood panel done, which showed nothing off other than high cholesterol which was fixed with diet.  The center of the discomfort is I would say right at the bottom of the sternum in the center, obviously you feel a pressure there you feel it in your chest and stomach somewhat, it feels like it really sets off my nervous system when its acting up. I notice a lot of tension in that area, I feel my heartbeat a lot if its up at all and it feels to be in that same spot where I feel it. If I pick up something even a 12 pound cat and hold it I get a bad pressure feeling in that and really feel my heart. Any gas or stomach discomfort seems to really set it off. Even having go #2 seems do it. It does seem positional sometimes, I do get some weird popping/vibrating in there, but it seems associated with gas/acid reflux a lot. It really makes me scared to excersize because its just a really scary feeling, but I want to get back into better shape, I know I am very out of shape because of feeling so bad.  I honestly feel like im going to pass out a lot too, but I dont, I never have, but I get the sensation like its coming on, that also makes me not sure how to even start excersizing. On top of that I've noticed in the last year or so im way more tired than usual, way more sore than usual. Im very heat sensitive when I never was before, I just dont know much of this is due to being out of shape, and how much of this is something going on or even the hernia, but I need to get over this because I need to get back into better shape for the future. Im already feeling like im 90 at 31.

column3: Adenovirus?
column4: Last Thursday I developed urethritis (medium pain, small clear/yellowish discharge). I had unprotected sex with a new partner about a week before. The urethritis hadn't gotten better by that Saturday, so I went to urgent care. Got a urine strip test for UTI (negative), and received 1g of Azithromycin and a Ceftriaxone shot, along with a urine culture.  Monday, I received the results of the culture - negative for chlamydia, gonorrhea and UTI. Scheduled an appointment with my primary care physician and went in Tuesday. They treated empirically for trichomoniasis with 2g Flagyl.   Thursday I developed conjunctivitis. Not particularly painful, but inflammation of the inside of both eyelids and bloodshot eyes.  As of right now the pain during urination has decreased from what it originally was (probably about 50%) but it's still there with the conjunctivitis. Groin was a bit tender around one of my lymph nodes originally, but that's gone now.  I've been reading online and it's been pointing me to adenovirus due to both the urethritis and conjunctivitis. Is this a possibility, and am I good to wait until Monday? Urgent care wasn't much help last time and I don't want to be stuck with a bill for that if they're just going to give me topical antibiotics for my eyes that don't do anything.

column3: 16 Weeks Pregnant and Abdominal/Cramping- Lactose Problem or Something Else?
column4: I (36/f/134 pounds) am 16 weeks pregnant with my first. Before pregnancy, I was healthy with no medical issues and had no issues with milk. I do not drink or use drugs and the only medication I take is a prenatal Vitamin, omega 3 tab, and a calcium/d3 tab daily. Starting at about 12 weeks into pregnancy, when I had cereal/milk I got very sick with stomach pains and abdominal cramping for 3-4 days. My intestines/colon/ bladder area felt swollen and I could feel it when walking resulting in me walking crouched over. My bladder would feel very full and slightly burning when there was a small amount of fluid in it. It eventually passed but at the time, I did not connect it to the cereal/milk as it was not new in my diet.   It happened a second time and I understood it was the milk. Again, the same cramping for 3-4 days and swollen abdominal area. I saw my doc and was tested for a bladder infection and all was normal. I had an ultrasound for the baby (it was already scheduled, not in response the pain) and baby was healthy, normal weight/heart rate and no visible concerns.   This is the third time now. I did not have milk but had hot chocolate powder with hot water which apparently had milk ingredients in the powder.  I have been experiencing pain since Thursday, starting about 7 hours after consuming the beverage. This is day five of symptoms. I have not had any diarrhea,fever, or vomiting in any incident, mostly bad pain and cramping in my lower abdominal area. My doc advised me to take Lactate to help digest milk proteins but otherwise gave no other advice.   Is this normal that an allergic reaction/lactose intolerance reaction lasts this long and makes my bowels feel like they are swollen to the point it hurts to get up, walk, sit, etc? Has anyone else experienced this or have an idea what this could be? With pregnancy I know things move slower through the digestive tract, maybe it\'92s taking a long time for the milk to clear my system? Any advise/ideas are appreciated.

column4: Hey all,   Cross posting here and to r/dermatology. column3: 25M Will Sleep Apnea Kill Me?
column4: **Height:** 174 cm (5.7 ft)   **Weight:** 73 Kg (160 lbs)   **Race:** Asian   **Nationality:** Lebanese   **Drink:** Rare   **Smoke:** Rare   **Drugs:** Hash(low consumption)  # Story Time  I've never visited doctors for check-ups in my life, until about a year ago, I visited a GP for my main symptoms that were:  * Constipation * Heart Burn * Slow Metabolism * Fatigue * Low Mood * Anxiety * Frequent Nasal Congestion * Lump feeling in throat * Excessive Loud Snoring * Low(est) Sex Drive.  We performed several blood tests and I was diagnosed with **Helicobacter Pylori** and Allergy(we didn't perform a test to detect on what exactly).  During that visit, the doctor checked my blood pressure and my heart beats, and was immediately frightened, he told me that my **blood pressure** was 16 and my **heart beats** are irregular(the second beat was heard twice because the 2 parts of the heart are out of sync), and he asked me to do a  Echocardiography.  The results showed that my **Septal Thickness** and my **Posterior Wall Thickness** were both at 11mm which he told me is considered bad for my age and there's probably something causing this enlargement.  He though I might have sleep apnea and I went and performed Polysomnography and I was diagnosed with **Moderate Obstructive Sleep Apnea** with a **Total** **AHI** of 16, with mean **Oxygen Saturation** of 95% and 87% on it's lowest.  # Medication & Treatment  I took medication for Helicobacter Pylori for 3 weeks, at the same time, I started using a **CPAP** machine after showing my sleep study test to a **Sleep Specialist.** First 2 weeks into the treatment I felt great, my metabolism drastically, I took a gigantic shit every day in the morning after I woke up, I didn't experience any heartburns, wet dreams were back and my sex drive sky rocketed. I was checking my blood pressure every day in the morning and it averaged at 122/65. My mood was lifted up to the sky and I couldn't be any more satisfied.  # Early Ending?  A while later things started slowing back again, metabolism mainly(no heartburn) and low mood. I started hating the CPAP machine as well after I truly realized that I have to deal with it for the rest of my life.  I suspected that my **Helicobacter Pylori** was back and I went to see a gastroenterologist and we did an upper endoscopy that came clear.  3 months into consistently using the CPAP machine(min 6 hours per night), I decided I want to ditch it for a day or two, and I never came back after I discovered that I'm no longer suffering from the symptoms, no fatigue and no sleepiness, I was feeling the same with or without.  # A While Later(A month ago)...  I'm anxious in general and I'm not super happy in my life, lately I've been working towards changing that, I started going to Psychotherapy to deal with my low mood, and I decided to give an **ENT Sleep Apnea Specialist** a visit to have a second opinion on my condition. He told me that my nose septum is good enough and after taking a look at my sleep study he told me that my sleep apnea is mild and there's no way it might be causing my high blood pressure and heart problems.  He prescribed some allergy medications for 30 days to get my nose clear at night, I took the opportunity to monitor my sleep behavior so I created an [Excel Sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1O6NFjkVgFBya1sCGb7Esi7fuY9GrW_e5_2XszdAxk1I/edit?usp=sharing) which in I kept track of my Blood Pressure(measured after I wake up in the morning), and of how many hours I slept and at what times, if I had dreams, my own sleep quality rating, if I took a nap and some other fun factors.  For the last 5 days as well, I tracked my sleep using an app which caught me snoring, however my snoring was lower in volume and way less intense than I remember the last time I heard it, but it's still there every day.  # One Million Dollar Question  I'm currently not satisfied with my mood, as well as with my metabolism if I don't' exercise regularly and I don't' eat reasonable amounts of fibers. I still have the **LUMP** feeling in my throat every now and then. My sex drive isn't that high as well. I'm also concerned about my **Blood Pressure** stats for the last month which are in the sheet(I don't know if early in the morning is the best time to check it or not, but they checked it 2 times at the hospital later in the day this month and first time was 142, second time was 134)  DOCTORS OF REDDIT, WHAT DO YOU THINK ABOUT THE STATS IN THE SHEET AND WHAT DO YOU ADVISE ME AFTER HEARING MY STORY?  Thank you very much for reading, I could really use some help now and it's much appreciated <3  &#x200B;  **TL;DR** | Should I be concerned about my blood pressure stats in this sheet?  [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1O6NFjkVgFBya1sCGb7Esi7fuY9GrW\\_e5\\_2XszdAxk1I/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1O6NFjkVgFBya1sCGb7Esi7fuY9GrW_e5_2XszdAxk1I/edit?usp=sharing)

column3: I have a 5cm fibroid tumor pressing on my bladder. Won't have follow-up for a while and I'm looking for a broad idea of what I'm looking at for treatment.
column4: I'm 31, female, 5'7", 232lbs, and Caucasian. I have IBS, migraines, gnarly dust allergies, and the fibroid in the title. I've never been a smoker. I had my Nexplanon removed at the same time as my surgery (5 days ago), and I was nearing the end of my second implant. Before that I used depo-provera for a couple of years. If you need other general medication, let me know.  I recently underwent a laparoscopic bilateral salpingectomy. While my surgeon performed this procedure, he discovered a 5cm fibroid that is pressing on my bladder. I didn't get a very detailed breakdown because I was coming out of anesthesia and my husband mostly heard "tumor" and then "almost definitely benign" and sort of blanked out the rest. (That wasn't my post-op care.) The surgeon did say that he would be surprised if I didn't have discomfort from it, and that doing something about it would be a bigger incision and longer recovery.   Other than that, I don't know particular details such as intramural/submucosal/etc, but I messaged the nurse and she's the one who told me the size and that it's on the "upper left 'horn' of the uterus." I do seem to have a bladder the size of a thimble the last couple of years, compared to my memory of my previous habits. I also get heavy, painful, long, and irregular periods. We had discussed taking a good look around while we were in there, because my mom has terrible endometriosis and I was concerned I might as well.  What I'm hoping for is information on treatment. If the surgeon is thinking bigger incision does that mean size/location requires an open abdominal prodecure?  Laparoscopic but with extra cutting? Is a hysterectomy the likely suggestion? I couldn't find even a general rule of thumb for when a fibroid is considered too large for laparoscopic myomectomy vs abdominal. I gather that 5cm isn't huge, but it doesn't seem that small either.  I'm obviously not concerned about preservation of fertility, but I do know that getting a hysterectomy younger can have more long term consequences. I'd like to keep my uterus unless there's a medical advantage to a hysterectomy in this case. I know I'm probably missing some important information, but thank you for any advice you can provide.

column3: Frequent inflammation seven months post-op from a microdiscectomy?
column4: 20M, 5\'928 and 161lbs  I had a microdiscectomy back on November 8th 2019 in the L-5, S-1 region for a herniated disk (right side of the spine) on the I had since August of 2018. I was unable to fully elevate my right leg while sitting down, as well as a slew of other common symptoms. All of these went away after I had the surgery. It healed up well and I was pain free. I started physical therapy later than recommend due to scheduling issues, so around mid January. Since then, I\'92ve been having 2-3 sessions a week, and continue to do so over video calls at home. It was going really well, but in mid February I ended up with an inflammatory response after pushing too hard during core strengthening exercises which lasted about a week. We got through it and got back to it but with some alterations. In mid March, we started to also work on getting my right leg back up to par with my left leg, and things were going smoothly, but unfortunately the clinic had to close due to the pandemic come April. Via video calls, we started focusing mainly on quad exercises and ended up causing inflammation again, which lasted about a week and then went away. The symptoms were the same as the first time. It was fine after a week, and we continued. I had another flare up in early May, which went the same as the other times in terms of duration and pain. I\'92ve been doing quad strength training up until a week ago, where we started to shift towards calves to work on my balance. This is all so I can start running again. During this strength training (from mid April up until now, I\'92ve gotten increasingly stronger, but it also results in a lot of stiffness in my back. We worked through it, but a week ago, I had a flare up again. I\'92m just now getting over it, and I\'92m not concerned about any of the symptoms specifically. I\'92m writing this because, throughout this whole process, I\'92ve been improving, but it seems as if my back gets more and more prone to inflammation every time it gets inflamed for a week. Is it possible that this general \'93fragility\'94 in my back will go away in a few months? Is it possibly permanent? It keeps setting me back, and I\'92m just concerned that this is how it\'92s gonna be from now on. No matter how much I strengthen my core and muscles used to protect that area, it still isn\'92t enough. I know there\'92s no concrete answer, but will this get better over time? Will my back always be this susceptible to inflammation? Any information, suggestions, etc would be appreciated. Thanks.

column3: Why am I not concussed?
column4: Hi I'm an 18 year old male. 5'6" 140 lbs. Asian if that matters. So yesterday morning I woke up at 12:50pm and was extremely dehydrated apparently. column3: Red hands and feet, maybe related chest rash
column4: So for a long time I've been experiencing two things.   Firstly, red hands and feet. I usually have cold hands. If they get anywhere from comfortable to genuinely hot in temperature, and they tend to go very red. Same with feet except they're usually more comfortable.  I work as a lifeguard, indoors, and it's quite hot indoors. I walk around barefoot or in flip flops, and it's very noticeable how red my feet and hands are. Doesn't seem to be splotchy. Secondly, after a hot shower or bath (I usually bathe), I experience a reddening upper chest and lower neck. Usually this is just reddened but occasionally it's splotchy like a rash.   I'm 17, male, 5'11", 140lbs, white British, this has been going a minimum of 2 years, probably much longer. No relevant medical history to note. Should I go to the doctor tonight? What should I do?

column3: Multiple tendon injuries that have been ongoing, worried
column4: Hello,   I\'92m not sure where else to turn to, but I feel like I have hit a wall, and would really appreciate any help. I'm 28, male, 5'11, and ~155 lbs. No smoking, very little drinking. Previously only had mental health issues.    A little over a year ago, I began to hit the gym consistently for the first time in my life. A few months, later I began to accumulate injuries, at which point I greatly cut down my frequency and intensity of exercise, yet all my pains persist.   First, it was patellafemoral pain syndrome, which was around last May.  Then it was tricep pain, right where the elbow ends and the tricep begins. Starting in June,  I began experiencing shoulder pain, and was diagnosed with biceps tendonitis. My wrist began experiencing pain, at which point it was diagnosed as wrist + TFCC Sprain, ECU/FCU tendonitis. Most recently around a 2-3 weeks ago, I began getting pain on my big toe. I think it may be extensor tendonitis, but it is not diagnosed yet. I am scared that there is something fundamentally wrong with me. I am healthy otherwise. I have used ice, rest, and PT,  to no avail. I have also had an MRIs done on my shoulder , knee, and wrist in July. For my knee pain, the radiologist reported it could be \'91mild IT-band syndrome\'92. For the wrist, an \'91ECU subsheath stripping injury\'92. For the shoulder, there was  no evidence of rotator cuff tendinosis or tear.   I am currently working with my PCP to see an rheumatologist. I\'92m not sure if there is a greater underlying cause here, but am simply trying to learn more about what is happening to me. I\'92ve researched some treatment options, and am trying to get a prescription for nitric oxide patches, as they have been shown to help tendon healing (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2505250/), along with exploring PRP treatments. My PCP was not comfortable giving me PRP treatments, as he thinks there may be an underlying cause of these injuries. When going to the hand surgeon for my wrist in October, he mentioned that I may be hypermobile.   Am I missing anything? Any ideas regarding the underlying cause would be really helpful, thank you!

column3: Quetiapine concerns (combination with Bupropion)
column4: Yesterday I got prescribed the a-typical antipsychotic Quetiapine(25mg) in addition to my usual medication Bupropion(150mg).  &#x200B;  It's good to mention I am 25yo woman, 43kg; I don't have bipolar, schizophrenia nor psychosis. I struggle with BPD, anxiety disorder and strong symptoms of OCD. I got this prescribed when I complained that although I am very happy with the Bupropion (Wellbutrin) helping tremendously with my depression and want to keep taking it, when I am triggered the rest of the areas I struggle with are not affected by this medication.  &#x200B;  The way my psychiatrist presented it was very chill. Oh a side effect is drowsiness, so take it before sleep, you may have a difficult waking up. I haven't been able to get a good night sleep since I can remember myself and so I was like woah that ain't bad.  &#x200B;  Psych generally did not mention much than this, other than "there is no scientific research to confirm it will help, but it's a common practice in clinical psychology, so I believe it will help you". Okay...? I go get it.  &#x200B;  Going towards the pharmacy from her office, I started reading about side effects and such. I found SO many articles/videos etc listing it as part of the medications you should avoid at any cost. Something I found was also that you can't eat grapefruits, which was strange too. Usually when researching antidepressants , there are always some bad reviews, but on this one the negative seems to be outweighing the positive.  &#x200B;  When I got to the pharmacy, I asked them to let me know what are the side effects, things I should or should not do while taking it, etc. I also mentioned the grapefruit thing. She looked at me and said no this is bullshit, just make sure to take it at night and not drink alcohol.  &#x200B;  I'm not gonna lie, this answer seemed so freaking dumb to me... I am annoyed that my doctor and pharmacist don't just help me with providing information especially on a medication which seems to be a bit worrying.  &#x200B;  What are your thoughts and concerns on it? column3: I'm developing random allergies, why?
column4: I'm 27F in the US, no medical conditions and taking no medications. I've never had any allergic reactions in my life, before all of this. (ETA: 5'10", ~135lbs, so I don't get deleted!)  Over the past 2 years, I have been developing very specific allergies to different products that I've never had any problems with before. I am curious about what causes adult-onset allergies to occur, and whether or not this is normal. .. I'm concerned that there is a bigger problem behind all of this :/ it's just so strange and sudden.  My specific reactions:  \'95\'95 It started with an allergy to chapstick with sunscreen in it, my lips blew up to 5x their normal size, then they blistered and peeled. I did not have this reaction to anything else with spf, just chapstick. Regular chapstick is fine.  \'95\'95 Then, I became allergic to Neosporin. Every time I use* it, I break out (only in the area applied) in a mild but angry looking rash of redness and a scattering of small red bumps. *(I try to avoid it, but every once in a while I forget about the reaction)  \'95\'95 Next, I developed a reaction to dishwasher detergent. When I am near a running DW, my throat immediately feels itchy, full, and tight. It resolves quickly when I leave the area. I have had this same reaction to many different kinds of DW detergent.  \'95\'95 Most recent development is nail polish. This is by far my worst reaction. Within hours of applying nail polish, my fingers and palms swell up, I get tiny, painful bumps around my nails and down my fingers, and the itchiness starts full-force. Over the next few days (and after removing the polish), the skin around my nails begins to blister, bleed, and scab up - the worst being *underneath* my fingernails, causing the nail to detatch from the nail bed - down to about halfway. It takes weeks to heal. Note: I've used nail polish on and off since I was young with no reaction, and this reaction now happens even with the same exact bottles of polish that I have successfully used multiple times before.  Thank you for any insight!

column3: Pain in left testicle
column4: Hello guys,  I am 21 years old, 60 kg. One month ago. I noticed a small amount of blood in my semen. It wasn't painful or anything, so I decided to leave it as it is, as I saw that this was something that could happen from time to time.  But as I wanted it to disappear as quickly as possible, I decided to stop wanking for some time. Exactly one week ago, I started to feel a very light pain in my left ball. It wasn't swollen, everything was quite normal, except the pain.  On Monday, as I thought the pain was because I didn't cum for a while, I decided to wank. Of course it was a LOT of semen, and I felt a strong pain just before the ejaculation.  On Tuesday, I immediately went to my GD. After examination of my balls, everything was normal. But he recommended a blood and urine test in order to be sure there wasn't anything.  On Wednesday, results of my tests: everything is good, there's nothing to worry about. Except that I noticed that my GD didn't specified to analyse the specified metrics for testicular cancer, which made me quite preoccupied.  On Thursday, the pain became a bit more intense. I called my GD, and as the results weren't indicating anything, he said I just needed to take painkillers.  On Friday, pain was still there, even with the painkillers. I called him for a second examination, and there he saw a small cyst in my ball. He said there's nothing to worry about, and that I just needed to take painkillers and that it would go away.  However, yesterday, as I was quite stressed with it and that I was still feeling pain, I decided to go to the ER. The doc examined my balls, said everything was normal and felt the cyst. He recommended to do a ultrasound, but thought that it wouldn't give any different results. A few hours later, he came to me with the results and said that the pain is in fact due to the cyst. He also said that I should take painkillers and that the pain will go away.  However, here I am on Sunday, still with pain. The pain isn't super intense, it isn't light either.. but I can feel it. Is it normal that even with medication, the pain is still there? What should I do more?  EDIT: I can't see any "lump" on my testicle, it hasn't swollen. The size of the cyst is 5mm, according to my GD and results of the ultrasound

column3: When do docs stop believing you?
column4: Hi. 26F, 130lbs and white with a dash of asian. Chronic pain starting in 2018 that I went to Mayo Clinic for and they couldn't diagnose. Family history of autoimmune, including sibling with Sjogren's Syndrome. The pain presents as idiopathic neuropathy everywhere - face, arms, legs, etc. and extreme photosensitivity -, though Mayo said it was neither neuropathy nor autoimmune. **All tests show up as if I'm perfectly normal.**  **My current and past PCP keep forgetting what meds I'm on or why I'm taking them so I want to switch, but I'm worried about looking like I'm doctor-shopping and then not getting help.** I have to keep telling her this med doesn't work, I'm still in pain. I've tried so many medications, none of which are opiates. I'm currently on 1200mg of gabapentin and 37.5mg of venlafaxine for pain. I have to message my doctor saying this doesn't work, I need an increased dose. I need to be able to function. I'm in grad school. I need to be able to work.  I'm scared that if I switch doctors too often it'll look like drug seeking behavior, especially since all of my tests come back clean. They're taking me at my word that I'm in pain, and I think the fact that I refuse opiates helps, but I'm just terrified of medical professionals not taking me seriously. I feel like my doctor already does.

column3: Could use some advice, I've been sick for a month and would appreciate it greatly.
column4: About the second week of January, I was experiencing some swelling in my throat and it was becoming difficult to swallow. I couldn't really tell what it was from looking in the mirror but I got to sticking my finger in the back of my throat to feel around and felt lumps on the very back of my tongue. I was also experiencing light nausea, was getting gassy and when I got gassy I would get really nauseous. When I would defecate, a white mucous was present in the stool, but not much. I got to looking online with the symptoms I was displaying and Candida Overgrowth and Thrush came up. I immediately cut out all sugar and lots of carbs from my diet. I noticed a big change instantly. In some ways I felt sicker at times but when I would go to the bathroom, even more white mucous would come out. I was also puking in the mornings and waking up in the middle of the night to puke and pass gas and/or defecate.  I went to urgent care when I got paid and the doctor there gave me Nystatin, which has helped a lot with the candida, and Ondansetron to keep me from puking. I was sick with it for about two weeks before I started really feeling better. Only, as I was coming out of it on week three, my roommate got the flu and got me sick with the flu as well. So I was down for yet another week with the flu.  I'm now getting over the flu and it seems I'm experiencing a little bit of swelling with my top left saliva duct. I'm not sure if this is normal or if I'm actually experiencing a different type of swelling with something else. Either way it's rubbing against my tongue, it's not painful but it is REALLY annoying and kind of makes me gag a little bit.  I'm not really sure what to do right now. I've lost about 30 pounds in four weeks and I just feel like my body is wrecked. I'm still eating and everything but my meals are smaller and I've not got much appetite. I'm supposed to go see my doctor in a couple of weeks and was thinking I might ask her for some help/advice when I found this subreddit today.  Anyways, I could use some advice on recovery and the swelling I'm experiencing.     EDIT 1 hour after posting: I'm heading to bed soon, I will check in the morning. Thank you.

column3: I'm a 21-year old male, Hispanic, 5'7'', 150-160lbs. I was at a new job today, and i ended up fainting withing 15 minutes into the job. I'm wondering if it was from the heat or over exertion? more info in comments.
column4: We were unloading tires from one truck into another and stacking them into columns of 5. The tires weighted around 80-90lbs. The outside temperature was very good since it was the early morning. I had done a couple of stretches before starting since I was already told it would be hard labor. As we start to unload them I want to make sure I look enthusiastic so start helping with putting them into columns, after placing the second tire it gets pretty hard to lift them that high, so another guy is helping me lift. I've never been really physically strong, but i can lift 50lbs and stuff like that, i have ok stamina. As we finish the second column, I start panting, i'm thinking it's just cause there is no ventilation in the back of the truck. My muscles start aching but i don't think it's anything too out of the ordinary with this type of work. As we work on the third column I can't lift as easily as i could before, so I hang back and to still be doing something i work on rolling the tires into the back. Even though i was close to the entrance of the truck the air i was breathing didn't feel refreshing and I was trying to take in bigger breaths. the last thing i remember someone asked me to help them lift another tire and then at some point i passed out. I was walked off the truck, sat down on the ground, and given some water. Prior to getting to work I had a glass of milk, a ham and cheese sandwhich, and a glass of water.

column3: [25M] Repeatedly waking up at night with a crushing feeling in my chest and strange readings on my wrist pulse meter

I'm not a smoker, and there's a family history of heart disease in my family. I've been vaccinated with Pfizer about two thirds of a month ago.  I also have severe anxiety. Here's a screenshot: https://imgur.com/a/Z899qzT  I'm currently on an island in the middle of nowhere. I'm going back home within the next 48 hours and I intend on seeing a doctor ASAP.
